title: Thai Red Curry

overview.description: A rich, fragrant Thai curry with tender chicken, creamy coconut milk and homemade Thai red curry paste.

method: Heat the oil over a medium heat.
Add the Thai Red Curry Paste.
Cook for 2–3 minutes until fragrant and the oil begins to separate.
Pour in half the coconut milk and simmer until slightly thickened.
Add the chicken and coat well in the curry paste.
Pour in the remaining coconut milk and the chicken stock.
Add the aubergine, red pepper, bamboo shoots and makrut lime leaves.
Simmer gently for 15–20 minutes until the chicken is cooked and the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the fish sauce and palm sugar.
Remove the makrut lime leaves if preferred.
Stir through the Thai basil just before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Thai curries balance salty, sweet, spicy and aromatic flavours rather than relying on heat alone.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to fry curry paste correctly, balance Thai flavours and finish a curry with fresh herbs.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick weeknight curry made possible by keeping homemade curry paste in the freezer.
chapman-tips: Cook the curry paste thoroughly before adding the coconut milk to develop its flavour.
One frozen portion of homemade Thai Red Curry Paste is the perfect amount for this recipe.
Thai basil has a distinctive aniseed flavour that differs from Mediterranean basil.
Taste and adjust the balance of fish sauce and palm sugar before serving.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze once cooled. Thai basil is best added fresh after reheating if possible.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently to avoid splitting the coconut milk.
